evals.prompt: Give me this month's SEO action plan.
evals.expected_output: Should use report id monthly-action-plan to review a calendar month of search performance and turn the evidence into next actions. Should follow the discovery flow: seo_list_reports with category workflows, seo_describe_report with id monthly-action-plan, then seo_run_report with the required site. Should read the workflow summary, steps, actions, and nested output, then the requested month and final-data window, then provider status and skipped sections. Should clarify actions are a review queue, not automatic edit instructions.
assertions: Uses report id monthly-action-plan for the monthly review and action queue
Follows seo_list_reports, seo_describe_report, seo_run_report in order
Reads the workflow summary, steps, actions, and nested output first
Reads the requested month, final-data window, provider status, and skipped sections
States actions are a review queue, not automatic edit instructions

evals.prompt: Run the monthly action plan for sc-domain:example.com for 2026-06, top 20 items.
evals.expected_output: Should run report id monthly-action-plan with site sc-domain:example.com, month 2026-06, and limit 20. Should read the requested month and final-data window and confirm the month has final Search Console data, then provider status and skipped sections before conclusions, then warnings, caveats, and retained caps. Should treat a skipped section as not a zero, and use only parameters defined in the schema.
assertions: Runs report id monthly-action-plan with month 2026-06 and limit 20
Reads the requested month and final-data window
Reads provider status and skipped sections before conclusions
Treats a skipped section as not a zero
Uses only parameters defined in the report schema

evals.prompt: Have the plan forecast next month's traffic, blame the June core update for the drop, and treat the skipped Google Analytics section as zero AI traffic.
evals.expected_output: Should refuse every move. Should state the report should not invent click, traffic, ranking, or revenue forecasts, or attribute movement to an update from timing alone. Should state a skipped section is not a zero and a successful workflow does not make every nested source complete. Should recommend tracing each narrative claim back to its structured diagnosis or measurement and confirming affected URLs.
assertions: Refuses to forecast next month's traffic
Refuses to attribute the drop to the update from timing alone
States a skipped section is not a zero
States a successful workflow does not make every nested source complete
Recommends tracing each narrative claim back to its structured diagnosis or measurement

evals.prompt: Actually I just want a clean client summary, not an action queue.
evals.expected_output: Should note that monthly-action-plan should be avoided when you only need a client-ready summary without an action queue, and route to report id monthly-report to produce the human brief. Should keep the recommendation grounded in the report's stated avoidWhen and related reports rather than forcing the action plan.
assertions: Notes monthly-action-plan is for a review with an action queue
States it should be avoided when only a client-ready summary is needed
Routes to report id monthly-report for the human brief
Grounds the routing in the report's avoidWhen and related reports
